proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +32.0pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.61 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance (project_anchor §7 D-8); true edge magnitude pending D-8 resolution
Portfolio regime concentration triggered at 0.859 in Choppy — if regime rotates to Bear, this cohort will activate against an inverted exposure profile and should be re-evaluated
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +32.0pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +32.0pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (stable, 7 days) with only 11 Bear exposures, so activation is deferred and the existing Choppy concentration (85.9%) is not immediately compounded. Consistent with multiple prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-07) with no trader rejection on record.

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +25.5pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +25.5pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.72. The sector=Metal filter narrows rather than amplifies the existing UP_TRI exposure (27 of 78 open) in a Choppy-concentrated portfolio, and TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental concentration. Consistent with multiple prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-07) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime = Choppy (85.9%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime = Choppy; recommended action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy until Choppy share drops below 0.80 via natural Day-6 exits, OR until Choppy×DOWN_TRI cohort promotes from Candidate to at least Tier W. Do not add new DOWN_TRI shorts in IT or FMCG sectors.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bull regime flip would simultaneously invalidate the entry premise for ~86% of the book; the 46 DOWN_TRI shorts (59% of book) would face coordinated stop-out pressure on a relief rally, with IT and FMCG short clusters (16 positions combined) hit hardest as defensive/growth sectors typically lead Choppy-exit rallies.

evidence: Dominant Choppy×DOWN_TRI cohort matched at Candidate tier only (n=15) — unvalidated edge
Both flagged cells (DOWN_TRI×Choppy×IT at 10.26%, DOWN_TRI×Choppy×FMCG at 10.26%) map to same Candidate-tier cohort
DOWN_TRI dominates signal mix at 46/78 = 59%, reinforcing short-side directional skew
Pattern persists from prior 4 consecutive warns (May 4–7) — concentration not naturally unwinding
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy until Choppy share drops below 0.80 via natural Day-6 exits, OR until Choppy×DOWN_TRI cohort promotes from Candidate to at least Tier W. Do not add new DOWN_TRI shorts in IT or FMCG sectors.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bull regime flip would simultaneously invalidate the entry premise for ~86% of the book; the 46 DOWN_TRI shorts (59% of book) would face coordinated stop-out pressure on a relief rally, with IT and FMCG short clusters (16 positions combined) hit hardest as defensive/growth sectors typically lead Choppy-exit rallies.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Regime concentration is triggered at 0.859 (Choppy dominant, above the 0.80 threshold), and the matched Choppy×DOWN_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=15) — unvalidated edge. While no single cell breaches the 20% threshold (top cells at 10.26%), the regime concentration alone with unvalidated cohort support warrants warn, and DOWN_TRI dominates signal mix at 46/78 = 59% reinforcing directional skew within an unvalidated regime.
